bibliographic_data.title: BiSeNet: Bilateral Segmentation Network for Real-time Semantic Segmentation
bibliographic_data.abstract: Semantic segmentation requires both rich spatial information and sizeable receptive field. However, modern approaches usually compromise spatial resolution to achieve real-time inference speed, which leads to poor performance. In this paper, we address this dilemma with a novel Bilateral Segmentation Network (BiSeNet). We first design a Spatial Path with a small stride to preserve the spatial information and generate high-resolution features. Meanwhile, a Context Path with a fast downsampling strategy is employed to obtain sufficient receptive field. On top of the two paths, we introduce a new Feature Fusion Module to combine features efficiently. The proposed architecture makes a right balance between the speed and segmentation performance on Cityscapes, CamVid, and COCO-Stuff datasets. Specifically, for a 2048×1024 input, we achieve 68.4% Mean IOU on the Cityscapes test dataset with speed of 105 FPS on one NVIDIA Titan XP card, which is significantly faster than the existing methods with comparable performance.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proposes a Bilateral Segmentation Network (BiSeNet) that decouples spatial information preservation and receptive field offering into two paths, achieving a balance between speed and segmentation performance.
core_content.key_methodology: The method involves designing a Spatial Path with small stride to preserve spatial information and generate high-resolution features, while employing a Context Path with fast downsampling strategy for sufficient receptive field. A Feature Fusion Module is introduced to combine the features efficiently.
outcomes: Achieved 68.4% Mean IOU on Cityscapes test dataset with speed of 105 FPS
Balanced between speed and segmentation performance
core_content.full_text_markdown: The research addresses the challenge in semantic segmentation by proposing a Bilateral Segmentation Network (BiSeNet). The network decouples spatial information preservation and receptive field offering into two paths: Spatial Path with small stride to preserve spatial details, and Context Path with fast downsampling strategy for sufficient receptive field. Additionally, Feature Fusion Module and Attention Refinement Module are introduced to further improve accuracy. The proposed architecture achieves 68.4% Mean IOU on Cityscapes test dataset at a speed of 105 FPS, significantly outperforming existing methods.
